description: The 'Ndrina Forastefano is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, Italy, operating in the Sibaritide area. It is involved in extortion, usury, vote-buying, drug trafficking, fraud, human trafficking, and infiltration of public contracts and local economic sectors. The group emerged from a split with the Abbruzzese clan and was victorious in a late 1990s-2003 feud against the Zingari of Lauropoli, gaining control over criminal activities in the upper Ionian Cosentino.
